deep_dive: payload byte-exact SRAM packing (reflects v3 spec 9.4 + 14 byte-exact candidate)
claim: for a published message, every SRAM address base..base+len-1 holds exactly the byte that belongs there (no gap / no offset / no overwrite / no loss)
technique: SYMBOLIC-BYTE formal: one (* anyconst *) address proves the content property for ALL addresses at once
lessons: Correct first FAILED formal: free-state messages longer than the 8-byte SRAM wrapped the 3-bit address and overwrote addr0. Fix reflects v3 10.2 MAX_MESSAGE_BYTES -> overflow drop (room guard); only fitting messages publish.
Then formal was UNKNOWN (true-but-not-k-inductive). Strengthened with auxiliary invariants (cnt <= DEPTH, wp == cnt) so k-induction closes -> a real unbounded proof.
The symbolic-byte technique proves a memory-content property over all addresses with a single anyconst, instead of enumerating every byte.
caveats: One byte per beat, base=0, DEPTH=8; production v3 packs into 256-bit SRAM words with 32-bit strobes and per-context partial words (next refinement).
Verilator lane scoreboards the exposed SRAM write port (anyconst is formal-only).
